2606.21295.title: Topological Neural Dynamics: A Neuron-wise Framework for Sequence Modeling
2606.21295.paper_summary: Existing sequence models, including RNNs, LSTMs, continuous-time networks, and Transformers, share a common structural principle: layer-wise dynamics, where all neurons in the same layer co-evolve through a shared parameterized operator, leaving individual neurons no freedom to evolve independently. Yet in many complex dynamical systems, rich global behavior emerges precisely from locally evolving units interacting through structured connectivity. Inspired by this principle, we introduce Topological Neural Dynamics (TND), a sequence modeling framework that shifts computation from layer-wise to neuron-wise dynamics. TND represents a neural system as a directed neuron graph, an interaction operator, and a local dynamics function, where each neuron evolves independently and collective computation emerges from interactions through the explicit graph topology. We instantiate TND as a discrete-time graph-coupled dynamical system and evaluate it as a case study on a behavior cloning task in single-player Pong. Compared with Vanilla RNN, Sparse RNN, LSTM, Closed-form continuous-time neural network (CfC), and Transformer baselines, TND achieves the best catch rate and a mean of 17.47 consecutive catches per round, more than three times that of the strongest baseline. These results suggest that shifting from layer-wise to neuron-wise dynamics provides an effective inductive bias for sequence modeling.
2606.21295.paper_summary_zh: 現有的序列模型，包括RNN、LSTM、連續時間網絡和Transformer，具有共同的結構原則：層級動態，其中同一層的所有神經元通過共享的參數化運算子共同演化，使得個別神經元無法獨立演化。然而，在許多複雜的動態系統中，豐富的全局行為恰恰是由局部演化單元通過結構化連接互動而產生的。受到這一原則的啟發，我們引入了拓撲神經動態（TND），這是一種將計算從層級動態轉變為神經元動態的序列建模框架。TND將神經系統表示為一個有向神經元圖、一個互動運算子和一個局部動態函數，其中每個神經元獨立演化，集體計算則通過明確的圖拓撲中的互動而產生。我們將TND實現為一個離散時間圖耦合動態系統，並在單人Pong的行為複製任務中進行案例研究評估。與Vanilla RNN、Sparse RNN、LSTM、封閉形式連續時間神經網絡（CfC）和Transformer基線相比，TND達到了最佳的捕獲率，每輪平均17.47次連續捕獲，超過最強基線的三倍以上。這些結果表明，從層級動態轉變為神經元動態為序列建模提供了一種有效的歸納偏差。
2606.21295.author: Borui Cai et.al.
